description.value:
You unleash a powerful uppercut that launches the target straight up, you then proceed to grab hold of the creature to slam them back downwards, allowing you to finish them off with a stomp.

This jutsu can be only be casted as either a Combo against a creature on the ground or as a Finisher against a creature who is currently midair, following the usual rules when doing so but granting different effects depending on how it was used.

If you casted this jutsu as a Combo, you make a melee taijutsu attack against a creature in range, on a hit you deal your [Unarmed Damage] + 3d6 bludgeoning damage and knock the creature 20 feet into the air until the end of the current turn. If you were to deal any damage to the affected creature as a result of a melee taijutsu attack or Hijutsu you cast, they instantly fall to the ground and take fall damage.

Until the end of your turn, you can target one affected creature with a Taijutsu or Hijutsu Finisher regardless of range, once per turn, using either an Action or Bonus action, ignoring its listed casting time.

If you casted this jutsu as a Finisher, you make a grapple check against the creature. On a success, you grab their face and bring them back down to the ground, having them take the result in fall damage if they don't already and knocking the target prone. You then land within 5 feet of the target, release the grapple and make a single melee taijutsu attack, on a hit you deal your [Unarmed Damage] + 5d8 bludgeoning damage.

At Higher Ranks: For each rank you cast this jutsu above C-Rank, increase the cost of this jutsu by 3, the damage by 2d6, and the distance you knock the creature into the air by 10 feet.
